page.title: Mounts & Pets
page.description: In Quinfall the only mount you actually own is the horse — donkeys, camels and elephants are rented from the Caravan Vendor for trade runs. Pets evolve and boost profession yield.

hero.title: 4 Mounts · 12-hour Breeding · 6 Pet Classes.
hero.body: Lasso a wild horse from the open world (only green or blue spawn there); donkey, camel and elephant aren't tameable — you rent them from the Caravan Vendor and run a caravan. Blue × Green takes 12 hours and can roll a Purple or Orange (Legendary) foal. Pets evolve to match your profession.

mounts.title: Four Mounts, Two Ways to Get Them
mounts.subtitle: The horse is lassoed from the wild and you own it. Donkey, camel and elephant aren't tameable — they're rented from the Caravan Vendor NPC to run caravans. Stats below distilled from in-game behaviour.

horse.name: Horse
horse.body: The only mount you can own personally in Quinfall. Spawns in the wild only in green (Common) and blue (Uncommon); purple (Rare) and orange (Legendary) come exclusively from breeding. The Spear (Lancer) deals double damage on horseback.
horse.tip: Buy your first lasso from the Mount Caretaker NPC. Blue horses are harder to find — worth the chase, they raise your breeding pool.

donkey.name: Donkey
donkey.body: Not tameable, not a personal mount. Rented from the Caravan Vendor NPC and used to form caravans. Slow but high cargo capacity; the backbone of starter caravan runs.
donkey.tip: Donkey-led starter caravans are the low-risk entry: cheap rent, simple route, steady gold.

ox.name: Ox
ox.body: Not tameable. Rented from the Caravan Vendor. Slower than the donkey but tops the cargo chart. Steady pace and high stamina — the long-haul half-sibling of the donkey.
ox.tip: Pick when the package is heavy and time isn't critical: solid gold-per-hour, more crates per run than a donkey.

camel.name: Camel
camel.body: Not tameable. Rented from the Caravan Vendor. The most efficient caravan animal in the desert: immune to the heat debuff, low water consumption. Mid-tier speed but in the desert it outpaces the horse.
camel.tip: If you're trading toward Horus, rent a camel — same route on a donkey is a stamina/time loss.

elephant.name: Elephant
elephant.body: Not tameable. Rented from the Caravan Vendor. Tops the chart on HP and cargo; weapons, bows and spells fire from the howdah on its back. In a caravan it's both transport and a moving combat platform.
elephant.tip: Elephant rent is the priciest option; without a guild caravan or a high-value route, donkey/camel return more gold.

tame.title: Lasso a Horse, Make It Yours
tame.subtitle: The horse is the only personal mount in Quinfall. Buy a lasso from the Mount Caretaker NPC, find a green or blue horse in the wild, and run the alignment mini-game to claim it.

wildHorses.title: Wild Horse Colours
wildHorses.body: Wild horses spawn at only two rarities. The higher tiers (purple, orange) come exclusively from the breeding pool.
wildHorses.note: Green horses are easy to find; blue ones spawn rarely and the chase is longer. Both are worth collecting — they feed the breeding pool.

npc.title: Mount Caretaker NPC
npc.body: Found in every city. Sells lassos and runs your stable. Don't confuse it with the Caravan Vendor — that's a separate NPC who rents donkeys, camels and elephants.
npc.bullet1: The lasso is cheap; affordable on a starter budget.
npc.bullet2: A captured horse goes straight into your stable; cap is 20 horses.
npc.bullet3: Mount Caretaker = lassos. Caravan Vendor = caravan rentals. Two different NPCs.

buyLasso.title: Buy a lasso
buyLasso.body: Cheap from the Mount Caretaker NPC.

find.title: Find a wild horse
find.body: Roam the open world; green is common, blue is rare. Slow down before you cross the horse's panic distance.

throw.title: Throw the lasso
throw.body: Get close enough, throw the lasso; on hit the mini-game starts.

minigame.title: Moving-arrow mini-game
minigame.body: Line up the moving arrow inside the target box. Right alignment tires the horse and it surrenders.

success.title: Capture complete
success.body: The horse goes straight into your stable, not your inventory. Summon it from the Mount Caretaker.

stable.title: Stable management
stable.body: Stable cap is 20 horses. To grow the breeding line, clear out older / lower-quality horses.

caravan.title: Caravan Vendor: Donkey, Camel, Elephant
caravan.subtitle: Donkeys, camels and elephants aren't tameable. You rent them from the Caravan Vendor NPC in any major city to form a trade caravan. Rent, package, route — all from the same NPC.
caravan.vendorBody: One of the main entry-point NPCs in any city. Pick a rental animal, summon the mount, buy a package, set a route to the destination city. Don't confuse it with the Mount Caretaker — that's the lasso vendor.

vendor.title: Vendor in the city
vendor.body: Every major city has a Caravan Vendor. You rent the animal and buy the package at the same counter.

rent.title: Rental window
rent.body: Rentals are time-bound; the animal is yours until you finish the run or the rental expires.

package.title: Buy a package
package.body: You buy the city's package from the same NPC. Package size scales with the rented animal's cargo.

route.title: Pick the route
route.body: Farther destinations pay more. Routes through dangerous land carry a bonus and the matching risk.

profit.title: Drop-off & payout
profit.body: Hand the package to the destination Caravan Vendor; payout hits the wallet on delivery. The animal can be returned within the rental window.

breeding.title: Green & Blue Roll Into Purple / Orange
breeding.subtitle: Wild horses only spawn green and blue. Purple (Rare) and orange (Legendary) come only from breeding the two together. Each cycle is 12 hours and the outcome is full RNG.
breeding.formula: Green × Blue → 12 hours → chance for Purple or Orange (RNG)

common.name: Green
common.body: Common. Plentiful in the wild — your first lasso target.

uncommon.name: Blue
uncommon.body: Uncommon. Rare spawns, longer chase. Critical for the breeding pool.

rare.name: Purple
rare.body: Rare. Only obtainable as a breeding result of Green × Blue.

legendary.name: Orange
legendary.body: Legendary. Top tier; the rare RNG outcome of the same pool.

duration.title: Breeding duration
duration.body: Both paired horses are unusable for 12 hours; you can't summon them until the cycle ends.

femaleCharges.title: Female mate slots
femaleCharges.body: Each female can be bred a total of 3 times. Once a female hits 3/3, she's done — out of the breeding pool.

maleUnlimited.title: Male mate slots
maleUnlimited.body: Males have no breeding cap; the same male can pair with different females again and again.

lockDuring.title: Lock window
lockDuring.body: While breeding runs, both parents are locked — can't ride, can't move, can't sell.

stableCap.title: Stable capacity
stableCap.body: Total cap of 20 horses. To expand a line, retire low-quality horses to free slots.

rngStats.title: Stats and colour
rngStats.body: The foal's stats and colour are fully random. Community-confirmed: there's no deterministic inheritance formula.

horseMedallion.name: Horse Medallion
horseMedallion.body: The base material required to breed Green (Common) and Blue (Uncommon) horses.

qualityHorseMedallion.name: Quality Horse Medallion
qualityHorseMedallion.body: Upgraded medallion required to breed a Purple (Rare) horse.

resetStone.name: Breeding Reset Stone
resetStone.body: Resets a female's used breeding charges back to 0/3 — you don't need to hunt for another female. Bought with V-Coin (Steam premium currency) from the in-game shop.

reductionStone.name: Time Reduction Stone
reductionStone.body: Cuts the active breeding timer by 6 hours. Saves serious time when you're racing for a line. Bought with V-Coin from the in-game shop.

premium.title: Premium Horses, Caravan Sets and Saddle Gear
premium.subtitle: V-Coin shop armoured cosmetic horses, top-tier caravan packs and the saddle + horseshoe gear that lifts your horse's performance. Every icon comes from the live game.

horses.title: Premium Armoured Horses
horses.body: Cosmetic horses unlocked through the cash shop or events. Stat-wise identical to standard horses; the difference is purely visual.

caravans.title: Special Caravan Animals
caravans.body: Beyond the standard Caravan Vendor rentals, top-tier caravan looks unlocked from events or the shop.

descriptions.desertCaravan: A standout cosmetic caravan set on the Horus route.
descriptions.sultansGuardian: Top-tier caravan look in Sultan-styled cosmetics.

gear.title: Horse Gear
gear.body: Saddles and horseshoes come in three quality tiers: higher quality lifts speed, stamina and ride comfort.

pets.title: Pets Confirmed in Quinfall
pets.subtitle: Active pet roster pulled from the in-game pet database. Filter by type; every icon comes straight from the game asset.

note.title: What pets do
note.body: Pets aren't decorative — they follow you, boost profession yield, and unlock a skill tree through evolution. The sections below detail evolution and the skill tree.

evolution.title: Evolve It, Open the Skill Tree
evolution.subtitle: Pets evolve over time; their look changes, their abilities sharpen, some morph into entirely new species. An end-game pet yields 2-3× a basic one.

level.title: Raise the pet's level
level.body: Pet XP grows as it fights beside its owner. Evolution doesn't unlock until a certain level.

material.title: Gather evolution mats
material.body: Engineer products, rare plants, or world boss drops are required. Varies by species.

npc.title: Visit the Mount Caretaker
npc.body: Trigger the ritual at a Mount Caretaker or a dedicated pet NPC.

ritual.title: Ritual and outcome
ritual.body: The evolution ritual runs; the outcome can be partly random (variant-bound). Some pets shift to a new species entirely.

skills.title: Pet Skill Tree
skills.intro: Time spent with the owner turns into points; points unlock pet skills.

gathering.title: Gathering Boost
gathering.body: Speeds up harvesting, mining, and lumberjacking. Non-negotiable for a lifeskiller.

combat.title: Combat Assist
combat.body: Joins the fight: takes aggro, tanks, or adds damage.

loot.title: Loot Detection
loot.body: Marks nearby rare drops. Direct effect on farm yield.

aura.title: Stat Aura
aura.body: Passive stat boost for the owner. The value climbs sharply on endgame pets.

skills.note: An end-game pet yields 2-3× a basic one — lifeskillers don't skip the pet investment.

strategy.title: Practical Paths for Pets and Mounts
strategy.subtitle: Core moves for new players, money lanes for the economy investor, weapon picks for the siege team, profession matchups for lifeskillers.

beginner.title: 🌱 New Player
bullets: Grab your first lasso from the Mount Caretaker; start by hunting green horses.
Run your first caravan with a donkey rented from the Caravan Vendor — cheap, simple route.
First pet: rabbit or fox; cheap with a gathering buff.
Catch every blue horse you see — gold for the breeding pool.

economy.title: 💰 Economy Play
bullets: Build a breeding pool: 2-3 blue females + 1 blue male.
If a purple foal lands, don't sell — feed it back into the pool to chase orange.
Buy a reset stone: revive a 3/3 quality female instead of replacing her.
Reduction stones: 12h → 6h, two attempts per female per day.

siege.title: ⚔ Siege Pet/Mount
bullets: Elephant rental: cannons/bows/spells from the howdah — a walking battlement, ideal for guild caravans.
Hunter Companion: extra tank to protect the healer.
Rhino pet: small frontline tank support.
Purple/orange horse + Lancer: devastating open-field flank combo.

profession.title: 🌾 Profession Pets
bullets: Miner pet: faster mining and higher drop rates.
Lumberjack pet: same idea, applied to trees.
Hunter pet: tracks mobs, surfaces hidden drops.
